title: ConformanceProjectionResponse
description: The outcome the conformance runner writes for one ConformanceProjectionRequest (spec §6.3.1): the Projector's view state after folding the request's `given`. The runner reads a JSON ARRAY of requests on stdin and writes a JSON ARRAY of these outcomes on stdout — one per request, IN THE SAME ORDER — then exits 0; array position is the sole correlation. The view is a flat JSON object mapping each view FIELD NAME to its value, and every value is a wire scalar (§3.3): `boolean`, 64-bit signed `integer`, or `string`; a field a projection schema declares `number` or `date` (§3.2) appears here AS A STRING. Equality against the expected view is FULL-STATE: the response must match the expected object exactly — an EXTRA field is as non-conformant as a wrong or missing one. Because the property NAMES are the view's field names (open-ended, not enumerable in advance), this schema cannot set `additionalProperties: false`; instead it constrains every value to the wire-scalar union via `additionalProperties`, and the full-state equality check — not this schema — enforces the exact field set. Self-contained: no cross-file `$ref`.

scalar.description: A wire scalar (§3.3): the only value types that appear in a view on this wire. `boolean`, a 64-bit signed `integer`, or a `string`. A view field declared `number` or `date` (§3.2) is carried here AS A STRING — the wire alphabet has no fractional-number or date type, so those cross as their string form.
